channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: EU pushes back hard against Trump tariff threats and his caustic comments that bloc is out to get US
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday pushed back hard against allegations by U.S. President Donald Trump that the 27-nation bloc was out to get the United States, and warned that it would vigorously fight any wholesale tariff of 25% on all EU products.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SCOTUS tosses challenge to TN law banning drag shows with kids present, state AG hails decision
item.description: Tennesee's ban on drag shows in public spaces where children are present will remain in effect as SCOTUS refused to hear a challenge to the law this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's why dozens of lawsuits seeking to quash Trump's early actions as president are failing
item.description: The Trump administration has been targeted by more than 80 lawsuits requesting emergency relief to stall the president's agenda, which judges so far have not granted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservative commentators seen with 'Epstein Files' binders after AG Bondi promises docs release
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conservative political commentators were spotted at the White House Thursday holding binders that read “The Epstein Files” hours after Attorney General Pam Bondi promised the release of documents about wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein, who sexually abused underage girls.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate committee recommends Lori Chavez-DeRemer's confirmation as Trump's labor secretary
item.description: A Senate committee voted Thursday to advance the nomination of President Donald Trump's choice to head the Department of Labor, one of the agencies named in lawsuits over moves by Elon Musk's cost-cutting team to access federal data systems.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: HUD terminates Obama-era housing rule that Trump warned would 'destroy' home values
item.description: The Trump administration rescinded the Affirmatively Furthering Fair Housing (AFFH) rule, which mandated that localities commission extensive analyses to certify that new housing developments do not exacerbate disparities among federally protected groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Giving US ownership of Ukraine's minerals would help keep peace: Hassett
item.description: Kevin Hassett said in an interview with NewsNation's Kellie Meyer that these minerals' estimated worth is $20B.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE access to US intelligence secrets poses a national security threat, Democrats say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic lawmakers are demanding answers from Elon Musk about whether staffers at his Department of Government Efficiency have shared national security secrets over insecure communication channels. Sen. Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts and Rep.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump meets with UK’s prime minister about drifting US support for Ukraine
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is visiting the White House to try to convince U.S. President Donald Trump that a lasting peace in Ukraine will endure only if Kyiv and European leaders are at the table as negotiations move forward with Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Live: Trump, UK's Keir Starmer speak after meetings
item.description: Starmer is expected to try to convince Trump that Ukraine and European leaders need a place at the table for peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump’s crackdown plays into misperceptions about immigrants and crime
item.description: The White House portrays its immigration crackdown as a success. Critics say the administration is trying to look tough for the cameras, and worry about the “tail wagging the dog.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: DFL, GOP team up on bills to battle absenteeism in Minnesota schools
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers this week are moving forward on five bills aimed at addressing chronic absenteeism in Minnesota schools, and DFLers and Republicans are working together on policy solutions.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Transgender troops are now being identified for removal under Pentagon orders
item.description: The military services have 30 days to figure out how they will seek out and identify transgender service members to remove them from the force.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department abandoning cases alleging discriminatory police and firefighter hiring
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is abandoning cases that sought to force police and fire departments to end what the Biden administration alleged were discriminatory hiring processes, Attorney General Pam Bondi said Wednesday in the latest move by the Trump administration to end government
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis, St. Paul join lawsuit against president over immigration enforcement
item.description: The White House argues St. Paul’s 2004 separation ordinance interferes with federal immigration enforcements, but St. Paul Mayor Melvin Carter said the city’s policy does not oppose lawful immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Chandra Colvin, MPR News

item.title: Out to Lunch: Speaker Demuth doesn’t want to be defined by one part of her identity
item.description: Earlier this month, Republican Rep. Lisa Demuth became the first Black person and female Republican to hold the role in the Minnesota House of Representatives. Minnesota Now sat down with her in a new series called, “Out to Lunch.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Should Kennedy seek to limit access to these medications, there are some avenues available to him in his new role.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign, saying they won’t help ‘dismantle’ public services
item.description: Twenty-one members of the United States DOGE Service have resigned, they said in an anonymous letter, citing DOGE’s ongoing work dramatically reshaping the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
